rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE JACK M. WONG RESUDIARY TRUST, DATED DECEMBER 15, 1996 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Change Of Venue
rulings.Rulings: [Part 1 of 2] This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Stephen M. Murphy. Before the court is Respondent John Wong's Motion to Change Venue ("Motion"). Per the Notice of Motion, relief is sought pursuant to Probate Code sec. 17000 et seq and Code of Civil Procedure sec. 392 et seq, specifically 396b. The Motion is DENIED, for the follow reasons. The Probate Code provides an applicable rule to determine the proper county for commencement of a proceeding under Trust Law (see Prob. C. sec. 1000). Under Probate Code section 17005, (a) The proper county for commencement of a proceeding pursuant to this division is either of the following: (1) In the case of a living trust, the county where the principal place of administration of the trust is located. (2) In the case of a testamentary trust, either the county where the decedent's estate is administered or where the principal place of administration of the trust is located. (b) If a living trust has no trustee, the proper county for commencement of a proceeding for appointing a trustee is the county where the trust property, or some portion of the trust property, is located. (c) Except as otherwise provided in subdivisions (a) and (b), the proper county for commencement of a proceeding pursuant to this division is determined by the rules applicable to civil actions generally. The Trust instrument at issue is submitted as an exhibit to the initial Petition, and to the Motion's Memorandum of Points and Authorities. Page fourteen, section Nine of the document states: "Ninth: Upon the death of the husband or wife while serving as trustee, or if the husband or wife shall resign or become unable or unwilling to serve as trustee hereunder, the other spouse shall serve as sole trustee." Although some language in the next subsection indicates the possibility of "successor trustee or trustees," the parties appear to agree the instrument does not provide an overt mechanism to appoint successor trustee(s) after the resignation or death of the surviving spouse and sole trustee. (see, J. Wong Decl. par. 7, 16, 17; Calhoun Decl. ISO Opp. par. 10, 11.) The parties also appear to agree that Respondent, following the death of Jack Wong in December 1996, assumed the role of co-trustee with the surviving spouse and trustee, his mother, and continued in the role as sole successor trustee after her death in November 2020. (Pet. at 2:11-21; MPA at 2:14-3:4; J. Wong Decl. par. 9-11.)

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE JACK M. WONG RESUDIARY TRUST, DATED DECEMBER 15, 1996 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR For The Purpose Of Posting Tentative Ruling
rulings.Rulings: [Part 2 of 2] The parties do not agree on the correct legal term to describe Respondent in relation to the Trust during the intervening years he has operated as a trustee - whether he is "Trustee de son tort" or "a de facto Trustee" (see Reply at 4:1 fn. 1). That determination is not before the court on this Motion. The disagreement belies the significance of what the parties do agree on in relation to this Motion: There was a defect in Respondent's assumption of the role of trustee which rendered it invalid, and "[Respondent's] appointment to the office of Trustee is not authorized pursuant to the terms of the Trust and requires court confirmation." (Reply at 3:24-25, 4:1 fn. 1, and 9:6-8; Pet. at 3:1-2; Amd. to Pet. at 4:15-17 and fn. 1.) There is currently no one appointed to the role of trustee either (1) pursuant to the terms of the Trust, or (2) by court order. The parties agree the Trust's real property assets are located in the City and County of San Francisco (see, Pet. at 3:5-6; J. Wong Decl at par. 16, fn. 2; Calhoun Decl. ISO Opp. par. 6). On that basis, and on the record before the court, San Francisco was the proper county for commencement of the instant proceeding, under Probate Code sec. 17005(b). Petitioner shall lodge with the clerk in Department 204 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order consistent with this tentative ruling as published above. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765 and passcode 514879. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765# and passcode 514879#. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204.
